raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2024 someone made a fraudulent purchase of a plane ticket using my JetBlue credit card information totaling in {$2100.00} ( transaction number XXXX ). I received an email on XX/XX/2024 about the transaction and I called Barclays Bank and disputed the charge. The plane tickets were purchased under my name from XXXX XXXX to XXXX XXXX then to XXXX for XX/XX/2024. Charges were removed while investigation was on going. 

On XX/XX/2024 I received a notification that the investigation was completed on XX/XX/2024 and they determined the activity was valid based on documentations provided by the merchant ( XXXX ). At this time the charges were posted again to my account On XX/XX/2024 I called XXXX directly to dispute the charge with them. They gave me the information of what happened. Tickets were purchased on XX/XX/XXXX to the location mentioned above, for XX/XX/XXXX, then it was canceled and rebook for XXXX to XXXX for XX/XX/XXXX and then was canceled again and refunded ( Not to my credit card ). The case was opened by the XXXX representative and I was given a confirmation XXXX  and was told to give them 7 days to investigate. They told me XXXX would reach out if more information was needed. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I called XXXX again to get an update. They were unable to give me an update and I was told to email XXXX, to see they could give me an update. I emailed them and I did not receive any response from them. I also called Barclays again and had them reopen the investigation On XX/XX/XXXX I called XXXX again to get an update and again I was not able to get any information. The representative stated he would have to resubmit the fraud case. I asked for some confirmation number or reference number on the case and he stated he didn't have a number to give me and I would have to just take his word that he submitted the claim.
